entries.definition:
كتاب الشين

شرث - شرج

(شرح) الشين والراء والحاء أُصَيْلٌ يدلُّ على الفتح والبيان. من ذلك شرحت الكلام وغيرَهُ شَرْحاً، إِذا بيَّنتَه. واشتقاقهُ من تشريح اللحم.

sh–r–ḥ to slice, to cut into thin pieces; to dilate; to expound; to manifest, to reveal, to lay open, to display. Of this root, only شرح sharaḥa occurs five times in the Qurʾan.
شرح sharaḥa a [v. trans.] to dilate, to ease up the constriction, to lay open (6:125) فَمَن يُرِدِ اللَّهُ أَن يَهْدِيَهُ يَشْرَحْ صَدْرَهُ لِلْإِسْلَامِ whosoever God wishes to guide, He (God) opens his heart to Islam [lit. expands his breast (i.e. the breast of the one He wishes to guide)]

* الشرح (also known as الانشراح) names of Sura 94, Meccan sura, so-named because of mention in verse 1 of ‘the Expansion’ of the Prophet’s heart to the Truth.
